text:
I watched sunlight flicker on water.

The light appeared. Disappeared. Appeared again.

I thought: what if that IS a logic gate?

That question became 28 verified claims,
a complete compiler, and a new computing paradigm.

🧵 The Photonic Logic Chip. Thread below. 👇

#photonics

text:
$POET v $MRVL

David Lazovsky is a mechanical engineer who has over 50 patents in semiconductors and photonics. He became Executive Chairman of $POET in 2017. Before his arrival, $POET focused on GaAs. It was Lazovsky who pushed $POET to develop their optical interposer. He left $POET in 2020 and created Celestial AI because he was more keen on developing the whole photonics ecosystem. The essence is how to move data around multiple logical chips and memory, optically. Optical Multi-Die Interconnect Bridge, US Patent #12399333 and Photonic Memory Fabrics, US Patent #12271595. His development at Celestial AI was based on the optical interposer from $POET. Celestial AI signed a purchase agreement with $POET. 

$MRVL acquired Celestial AI in Feb 2026, and Lazovsky became a senior member of $MRVL. This is when the problem started, I think. $MRVL didn't spend $3.25 billion on outsourcing one of the key components in their future business. They wanted a vertically integrated system.

Lazovsky must know the gory details of $POET's optical interposer. And yet, it belongs to $POET. It looks like $MRVL decided to develop their own interposer instead of outsourcing it from $POET. The incident, breach of NDA, was just an excuse, I believe. Because the partnership between $POET and Celestial AI was known for years.

$POET lost an exceptional business opportunity. They need to find another. $MRVL and Lazovsky need to come up with a different solution to make his system work without $POET's interposer. He is a smart guy and he may have an idea already. But will it be different enough for him to be able to avoid an IP battle? $POET looks screwed, $MRVL is getting into a risky business development. But somehow I don't find this will kill $POET, because their optical interposer can work in a different setting and doesn't depend on Lazovsky's fabric. It's the other way around at the moment.

text:
Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE. The SATCOM Wildcard Nobody's Talking About 👇

Everyone's focused on Sivers as a silicon photonics / data center play. Fair. But there's a second engine quietly building underneath and it could be massive.

👉Meet ALLSPACE

They build flat-panel, multi-orbit SATCOM terminals for defense and commercial use, maritime, mobility, fixed sites. Think Starlink-grade connectivity, but hardware-agnostic across LEO, MEO and GEO.

And Sivers is their chip supplier.

Here's where it gets interesting. Each ALLSPACE terminal uses up to 1,800 Sivers chips. At up to $10,000 per terminal, the revenue math scales fast:

- 1,000 units → $10M
- 5,000 units → $50M
- 10,000 units → $100M

Volume ramp expected: H2 2026

For context, Sivers' entire 2024 revenue was ~$30M. A few thousand ALLSPACE terminals could materially move the investment case.

This is what "small volumes, big numbers" actually means in semiconductor land. You don't need mass consumer scale. You need the right customer, the right ASP, and the right timing.

But there's a second engine quietly building underneath and it could be massive. 👉Meet ALLSPACE They build flat-panel, multi-orbit SATCOM terminals for defense and commercial use, maritime, mobility, fixed sites. Think Starlink-grade connectivity, but hardware-agnostic across LEO, MEO and GEO. And Sivers is their chip supplier. Here's where it gets interesting. Each ALLSPACE terminal uses up to 1,800 Sivers chips. text:
From our $SIVE report last night. 

Remember, we keep our reports conservative. 

Once you layer on pro‑forma net cash from the April raise, in‑place Wireless segment earnings, and legacy CST/InP photonics, total enterprise value steps up to ~SEK 4.16B (≈SEK 13.67/share).

With a further SEK 800–1,400M of unmodeled upside from IRIS², a second LiDAR win, an EU sovereign fab, and Marvell pull‑through.

Which collectively justify the convexity‑adjusted 12‑month EV range that underpins the SEK 41–45 per‑share target.

text:
It’s majority institutions moving $AXTI at these valuations, retail shorts might turn out terribly wrong.

If AXT goes down from export controls the photonics buildout does too. (Eg. AXT -> IQE -> Macom/lite) and there are bigger problems to worry about. 

If you just split out AXT’s 10+ upstream JVs into one merchant Western company it would probably be worth more strategically than 5N. 

Also revenue numbers are misleading since these are TTM contracts, not new ones signed. All the upstream materials like 7n high purity indium has spiked immensely since last year. 

It’s all vertically integrated so there might be a NAND style price hike off limited substrates, but unlike Sumitomo, AXT does the entire supply chain minus a few like red phosphorus. 

So it’s more dangerous than you think.

text: Huge beat on $QCOM could be signaling the bottom of the Smartphone Market, where it makes up a third of $GFS Revenue, could explain part of the surge in Volume seen on their OBV chart, instead of just a bet on the Photonics market; $GFS OBV has once again finished above it's Dec'22 ATH level, if it continues holding above past earnings, this stock will see ATHs again soon.
embedded_text:
If $QCOM sees a bottom in smartphone, it means there is also a bottom for $SOI and $GFS's core business.

If these see the weakest part of their business rebound while the bull thesis relies on photonics, there's no limit to what I'll tell my boss in a few months. https://t.co/KGNaFypf4D

For the first time, researchers at QuiX Quantum have demonstrated below threshold error mitigation in a photonic quantum computer. They used a technique called photon distillation, where multiple imperfect photons are combined through quantum interference to produce a cleaner, more identical photon. On a 20-mode photonic processor, this method reduced photon indistinguishability errors by 2.2× and achieved a 1.2× net reduction in total system error. That matters because quantum systems usually introduce more errors than they fix. This flips that balance. text:
CPO, SILICON PHOTONICS, AND OPTICAL INTERCONNECT

CPO IS MOVING FROM R&D TO EARLY PRODUCTION, BUT THE NEAR-TERM DOLLARS REMAIN SMALL RELATIVE TO THE STRATEGIC SIGNAL (READ-THROUGH 10)

Call support: Management said FormFactor is “now beginning to ramp our TRITON production test system, co-developed with Advantest and Tokyo Electron,” and that the ramp is accelerating. Management attributed the acceleration to “growing volumes of CPO chips planned for later this year” and FormFactor’s leadership in Test Insertion 1, which ensures known-good die on the photonic integrated circuit wafer. Management also cautioned that CPO remains in “the very early innings” and that other insertions are “proving to be difficult for customers to implement in production.”

Affected companies: Advantest Corporation (6857: Japan) and Tokyo Electron Ltd. (8035: Japan) are positively affected with low near-term magnitude and higher long-duration optionality. The transmission mechanism is direct: TRITON was co-developed with these partners, and early CPO production test creates future attach opportunities for ATE, probers, and co-developed test platforms.

Broadcom Inc. (AVGO: US), Marvell Technology Inc. (MRVL: US), Coherent Corp. (COHR: US), Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE: US), and Fabrinet (FN: US) are positively affected with low near-term but potentially meaningful long-duration magnitude. The mechanism is validation of silicon photonics and CPO moving toward production use cases in AI networking. If CPO becomes necessary to address bandwidth density and power constraints, optical component suppliers, silicon photonics developers, and AI networking ASIC vendors benefit from a larger production ecosystem.

Teradyne Inc. (TER: US) is mixed to modestly positive. Management emphasized an open ecosystem and specifically said FormFactor works closely with Teradyne, but the named TRITON co-development partners were Advantest and Tokyo Electron. The mechanism is that Teradyne can still participate in future CPO insertions, but the earliest named production-test system appears more closely tied to Advantest and Tokyo Electron.

Near-term trading catalyst: positive for CPO sentiment but limited in revenue magnitude. The call supports the view that CPO is no longer purely a lab topic, but it does not indicate that CPO is yet a large 2026 revenue category for the broader supply chain.

Longer-duration fundamental shift: potentially high. The critical point is that Insertion 1 is focused on validating known-good photonic integrated circuits before expensive downstream packaging steps. If this becomes a standard production insertion, optical probing and electro-optical test can become recurring infrastructure layers in AI networking manufacturing.

DOWNSTREAM HARDWARE AND OEMS

AI SUPPLY-CHAIN TIGHTNESS IS POSITIVE FOR SEMICONDUCTOR VENDORS BUT NEGATIVE FOR HARDWARE OEM MARGIN FLEXIBILITY (READ-THROUGH 11)

Call support: Management described DRAM as supply constrained, HBM demand stepping up, networking demand strong, data-center CPU probe demand improving, CPO chip volumes planned later in 2026, and probe-card capacity constraints across FormFactor and competitors. The call also noted isolated cases where customers are willing to pay expedite fees in a capacity-constrained environment.

Affected companies: Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL: US), Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. (HPE: US), Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI: US), Lenovo Group Ltd. (0992: Hong Kong), and HP Inc. (HPQ: US) are negatively affected with low-to-moderate magnitude on margin and delivery-risk grounds, while remaining positively exposed to AI server demand. The transmission mechanism is input-cost and availability pressure. If DRAM, HBM, probe cards, and advanced-packaging capacity remain tight, OEMs and server integrators face higher component costs, longer lead times, and reduced ability to optimize gross margin.

Apple Inc. (AAPL: US) is modestly negatively affected through memory availability and potential LPDDR/DRAM cost pressure. The mechanism is less directly AI-server related but still relevant if DRAM wafer starts are dynamically allocated across HBM, DDR5, DDR4, and other designs to maximize supplier margin.

Near-term trading catalyst: negative for gross-margin assumptions at hardware OEMs if memory price increases and AI component shortages persist.

Longer-duration fundamental shift: mixed. AI infrastructure demand supports server revenue growth, but the profit pool may continue migrating upstream toward memory suppliers, advanced-packaging providers, foundries, and test suppliers rather than downstream assemblers and OEMs.

CONSUMER SEMICONDUCTOR RECOVERY IS NOT THE DRIVER; AI INFRASTRUCTURE IS ABSORBING THE INCREMENTAL TEST AND CAPACITY DOLLARS (READ-THROUGH 12)

Call support: Management described Q2 Foundry & Logic growth as driven primarily by data-center CPU strength, with continued networking strength and “steady demand in PC and mobile.” The word “steady” is important because it contrasts with the stronger language used for HBM, networking, GPUs, custom ASICs, and CPO.

Affected companies: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM: US), MediaTek Inc. (2454: Taiwan), Qorvo Inc. (QRVO: US), Skyworks Solutions Inc. (SWKS: US), and other mobile-exposed semiconductor suppliers are neutral to modestly negatively affected from a relative-growth perspective. The transmission mechanism is capital and capacity allocation. The call suggests incremental advanced test and probe demand is being pulled toward AI infrastructure rather than broad PC or mobile demand acceleration.

Apple Inc. (AAPL: US), HP Inc. (HPQ: US), and Lenovo Group Ltd. (0992: Hong Kong) are neutral to modestly negatively affected as end-market read-throughs. The mechanism is that upstream test demand is not indicating a strong consumer device rebound; PC and mobile are stable contributors, not the source of upside.

Near-term trading catalyst: negative for broad consumer semiconductor beta if investors are looking for evidence of a synchronized semiconductor recovery. The FormFactor call supports a narrower AI-led cycle.

Longer-duration fundamental shift: positive for AI infrastructure concentration and negative for the relative importance of consumer electronics in leading-edge semiconductor capital allocation. The call reinforces that the incremental complexity dollars are accruing to HBM, AI networking, CPU inference, GPU, custom ASIC, CPO, and advanced packaging rather than to traditional PC/mobile cycles.

text:
#SIVE $SIVE #5G #6G #Beamforming #FWA #SATCOM #CHIPSAct #Semiconductors #Wireless #Nokia

Take a moment to read this, friends — we’re going to talk about the unknown side of Sivers.

A lot of investors — and me first — only knew Sivers for its photonics business (the lasers for AI datacenters). 

It’s exciting and has massive potential. But after digging a bit deeper, we discover a real hidden gem: the Wireless division.

 It still accounts for ~70% of current revenue, delivered very strong growth (+85% on some quarters in 2025), and provides solid cash-flow stability.

 It’s the quiet but powerful pillar that makes the entire $SIVE investment thesis much less risky.

First of all, what is a beamformer? (simple explanation) : 

Imagine you want to send a super-fast internet signal (5G, future 6G, or satellite connection) to a phone, a house, or a satellite.

A regular radio signal spreads out in all directions, like a light bulb lighting up an entire room. It works, but it’s very inefficient. You waste a lot of energy, the signal weakens quickly over distance, and there are lots of interferences with other devices around.

A beamformer (literally “beam former”) is like an ultra-precise smart projector:
Instead of spreading everywhere, it concentrates all the signal power into a very narrow directional beam pointed exactly at your phone or your house.

Result? The connection is much faster, much more stable, reaches much farther… and uses way less energy.

Sivers Wireless develops exactly these mmWave beamformers (chips + antenna arrays). 
This technology is essential for high-speed 5G/6G, Fixed Wireless Access (FWA = wireless internet instead of fiber), SATCOM, and defense applications.

Major wins to date (April 2026):

• January 2025 → $5.4M contract (60 M SEK) signed with a global Tier-1 ( The crowd is murmering Nokia ? ) 

It’s for the next-generation beamforming transceivers. Even better: the collaboration is planned across multiple generations.

• New → Firm $3M purchase order with Tachyon Networks (28 GHz FWA). Production and shipments already starting in 2026 — real revenue incoming.

• 3 design wins in 5G/FWA already secured → potential of around 1 million units over the first three years of production (starting 2026).

• India → Historic partnership with Intel + WiSig Networks + office opened in Bangalore to target the massive FWA market.

• Defense → CHIPS Act funding (2nd round ongoing), contracts with Raytheon, BAE Systems, Ericsson. Daybreak™ 7-15 GHz ICs now available (March 2026) — very competitive in power and efficiency.

• SATCOM → Cloudchaser chipset + Maverick antenna arrays launched (Feb 2026) + strong commercial interest.

• 6G → Already involved in ISAC research with Nokia.

Why is this strategic for $SIVE?

Wireless brings stability and predictable revenues while the AI photonics division ramps into mass production (2026-2027). It’s the financial cushion that lets the company breathe… and it has strong potential to become a real cash cow from 2026-2028.

And even if photonics hits some delays or doesn’t deliver as expected, the Wireless division alone is strong enough to justify a major re-rating on its own.

In short: $SIVE is not just an AI laser story.

With the recent news of the Photonics spin-off to Silicon Valley, $SIVE (Sweden) is effectively becoming a pure-play leader in Wireless/SATCOM/Defense, while still holding the majority of the US AI laser powerhouse. It’s the ultimate value unlock.

It’s also a powerful play in mmWave 5G/6G, SATCOM, defense, and emerging markets — with technology that is already generating revenue.

I hope this clarified some things and/or brought new info for the Sivers community!

This is not financial advice. I’m just digging into this company and sharing what I found.

3238}, {"from_index": 3710, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 3800}]}, "text": "#SIVE $SIVE #5G #6G #Beamforming #FWA #SATCOM #CHIPSAct #Semiconductors #Wireless #Nokia Take a moment to read this, friends — we’re going to talk about the unknown side of Sivers. A lot of investors — and me first — only knew Sivers for its photonics business (the lasers for AI datacenters). It’s exciting and has massive potential. But after digging a bit deeper, we discover a real hidden gem: the Wireless division. It still accounts for ~70% of current revenue, delivered very strong growth (+85% on some quarters in 2025), and provides solid cash-flow stability. It’s the quiet but powerful pillar that makes the entire $SIVE investment thesis much less risky. First of all, what is a beamformer? (simple explanation) : Imagine you want to send a super-fast internet signal (5G, future 6G, or satellite connection) to a phone, a house, or a satellite. A regular radio signal spreads out in all directions, like a light bulb lighting up an entire room. It works, but it’s very inefficient. You waste a lot of energy, the signal weakens quickly over distance, and there are lots of interferences with other devices around. A beamformer (literally “beam former”) is like an ultra-precise smart projector: Instead of spreading everywhere, it concentrates all the signal power into a very narrow directional beam pointed exactly at your phone or your house. Result? The connection is much faster, much more stable, reaches much farther… and uses way less energy. Sivers Wireless develops exactly these mmWave beamformers (chips + antenna arrays). This technology is essential for high-speed 5G/6G, Fixed Wireless Access (FWA = wireless internet instead of fiber), SATCOM, and defense applications. Major wins to date (April 2026): • January 2025 → $5.4M contract (60 M SEK) signed with a global Tier-1 ( The crowd is murmering Nokia ? ) It’s for the next-generation beamforming transceivers. Even better: the collaboration is planned across multiple generations. • New → Firm $3M purchase order with Tachyon Networks (28 GHz FWA). Production and shipments already starting in 2026 — real revenue incoming. • 3 design wins in 5G/FWA already secured → potential of around 1 million units over the first three years of production (starting 2026). • India → Historic partnership with Intel + WiSig Networks + office opened in Bangalore to target the massive FWA market. • Defense → CHIPS Act funding (2nd round ongoing), contracts with Raytheon, BAE Systems, Ericsson. Daybreak™ 7-15 GHz ICs now available (March 2026) — very competitive in power and efficiency. • SATCOM → Cloudchaser chipset + Maverick antenna arrays launched (Feb 2026) + strong commercial interest. • 6G → Already involved in ISAC research with Nokia. Why is this strategic for $SIVE? Wireless brings stability and predictable revenues while the AI photonics division ramps into mass production (2026-2027). It’s the financial cushion that lets the company breathe… and it has strong potential to become a real cash cow from 2026-2028. And even if photonics hits some delays or doesn’t deliver as expected, the Wireless division alone is strong enough to justify a major re-rating on its own. In short: $SIVE is not just an AI laser story. With the recent news of the Photonics spin-off to Silicon Valley, $SIVE (Sweden) is effectively becoming a pure-play leader in Wireless/SATCOM/Defense, while still holding the majority of the US AI laser powerhouse. It’s the ultimate value unlock. It’s also a powerful play in mmWave 5G/6G, SATCOM, defense, and emerging markets — with technology that is already generating revenue. I hope this clarified some things and/or brought new info for the Sivers community! This is not financial advice. I’m just digging into this company and sharing what I found. #SIVE $SIVE #5G #6G #Beamforming #FWA #SATCOM #CHIPSAct #Semiconductors #Wireless #Nokia"}}}, "previous_counts": {"favorite_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2049610156166570331", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "389", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Daily Recap: The historic run continues
- $QQQ 21.6% in 23 sessions followed by the $SPY 15.2% and the $IWM at 17%
- $XLK continues to be the market dominant group. $AAPL very near an all time high with the obvious horses being $INTC $AMD $MU. 
- Photonics work up once again after being quiet for a few weeks. $AAOI $CIEN $LITE $VIAV $LWLG $AXTI. The group is due up next for earnings this upcoming week. 
- Software $IGV is also giving the impression that we may have bottomed. Some names are already leading that group and on Thursday we had a great earnings reaction and follow through from $TWLO. $DOCN $FSLY $RNG $FIVN $BAND $NBIS ones to watch. 
- $XLE was still the leading group for the week and it's hard to write those names off when you look at the structure on many of those stocks. $CRAK $BOAT $OIH a few places to watch over the upcoming weeks. 
- $BTC $ETH stage 4B setups unfolding with $CRCL being the only real place of interest for me for now. 
- Memory names are being lead by 4 incredibly powerful trend that show no real signs of slowing. $SNDK $STX $MU $WDC. 
- We got through major earning reports last week and this week is also an important one. Focus on names gapping up on large volume and watch the for continuation setups. $MXL is a great example. 
- Keep universe lists updated and I will see you all on Monday!

text:
Memory and photonics have already run hard, but Layer 5 (Capacity Operators) has the biggest catch-up left. 🤖

$DGXX, sitting at only $240M micro-cap, looks like one of the cleanest pure-play #GPUaaS rare setups in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

Most peers are still years behind - stuck in permitting, transformer shortages, and grid upgrades.

DGXX has already completed the hard pivot: owns 70 MW approved power + commissioned ARMS 200 modular Blackwell pods at Alabama. This is literally the fastest way to turn power into revenue with scarce live Blackwell GPUaaS capacity.

Balance sheet is rock-solid: zero debt, ~$93M strong liquidity with no forced dilution. 

The stock has gone through classic micro-cap volatility. Now it sits at a clear inflection point.

First anchor GPUaaS deal signed - $19.6M SubQ AI revenue goes live May 15. This proves the model works and derisks everything. Advancing hyperscaler discussions with customers desperate for immediate capacity.

Superstar team with hyperscaler DNA:  Venkat Rangasamy (ex-Oracle AI Factories) as VP AI Infrastructure, @hansvestberg (ex-Verizon CEO) co-founding USDC, @GerardRotonda (ex-CFO Deutsche Bank) on the board. Talent this caliber only joins a microcap when they see something truly special.

The recent #USDC spinoff is brilliant and shareholder-friendly: with subsidiary entity raises dedicated capital for pods & GPUs separately - no dilution or debt for DGXX parent entity. DGXX keeps 55% ownership and the majority upside.

Roadmap with high visibility: 10 MW by Q3 2026 → ~$137–152M ARR + colocation → $11–$30/share (4-9x from here). Full 70 MW Alabama scaling → $7B–$12B+ market cap upside.

It’s not if but how fast - hyperscalers are hungry for immediately available power and capacity, and DGXX already has it. And, this is just the beginning of a multi-year AI capacity supercycle - demand for live power and GPUs will only keep growing.

$DGXX reminds me of the early-stage #IREN/#CIFR, both rerated 5-10x+ from pre-contract lows - the risk/reward looks exceptional from here, and it is still completely under the radar.

This could be us retail’s chance to get ahead before market finally connects the dots 🔥 (institutions flood in and analysts raise guidance.) 

Disclaimer: not financial advice. Always DYOR.

text:
The tools split because the physics spans 4-5 orders of magnitude in wavelength. An RF antenna is centimeters. A photonic waveguide is nanometers. 

Tackling both in one numerical simulation would be computationally nuts, so we got HFSS for RF and Lumerical for photonics.

Deep learning models can learn an embedding over EM fields that isn’t frequency-constrained.  We can tackle RF and photonics with the same model.

That’s actually what we’re building at @arenaphysica

text:
How is $KOPN weaved into all of this photonics, AI, datacenters talk?!

Kopin is a direct play on AI data center photonics / optical interconnects: 

Neural I/o repurposes Kopin’s proprietary MicroLED pixels (via their patented bi-directional NeuralDisplay architecture) as ultra-high-speed optical transceivers. It moves data using photons instead of electrons, replacing dense copper wiring and traditional laser-based systems between GPUs, processors, boards, and racks in AI/smart data centers.

This addresses the exact pain points (power, bandwidth, heat, scalability) that drive CPO demand. It promises the same (or better) performance with a fraction of the power per bit and eliminates expensive laser modules—positioning it as a disruptive, lower-power alternative/complement to the laser-heavy CPO stack ($SIVE’s InP DFB laser arrays, $POET’s interposers, etc.).

https://t.co/jZIG8z9CBJ has placed a $15M initial purchase order to fund the demonstration chipset. Kopin owns 19.9% of https://t.co/jZIG8z9CBJ and is the exclusive manufacturer of the Neural I/o chipsets. Kopin emphasizes its U.S.-based MicroLED manufacturing for secure supply chains (a big plus in AI/defense).

Stifel recently reinstated coverage with a Buy and $5.50 target; the stock has shown momentum on the news.

Kopin is entering the same optical interconnect layer for AI infrastructure with a novel MicroLED approach that sidesteps some of the traditional laser challenges. This reinforces another high-upside, U.S.-centric player in the photonics boom for AI data centers + defense. LONG $KOPN $SIVE

text:
Photonics, Optics & Memory stocks saw another massive breakout to new all time highs last week. 

Many of these stocks are up well over 100%+ year to date.

After the recent mag7 earnings reports; I think data centers/compute providers will be the next sector to run big.

$NBIS - $CRWV - $CIFR And $IREN

On the various Mag7 earnings calls, they noted their current bottleneck wasn’t the availability of chips, but the availability of compute & power/energy. 

There are smaller data center co’s like $SNLH $BITF $APLD - but the 4 I mentioned earlier are my favourites. 

Trade idea;
Wait for a breakout above previous highs for an entry, or a re-test of this descending triangle breakout.

Nobody listened to me about;

$AXTI at $26 before it flew 175% 
$AAOI at $80 and it ran 90% (so far)
$MU at $156 and it launched 200%
$SNDK at $250

text:
$IBKR – here’s how to get real-time market data for any international exchange on IBKR

After going deep on photonics bottleneck plays outside the U.S. (thanks Serenity), I kept running into the same problem: no real-time data. Japan, Taiwan, UK, France, Germany, Sweden ($SIVE right?) – all delayed or missing.

You have two options: buy at market price blind, or pay $1-2/month per exchange for live data.

I went with the latter. Here’s how to set it up.

Step-by-step guide:

1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon)
https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions
https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific
4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel)
https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc.
6.Add it. Most non-U.S. exchanges run $1-2 USD/month.
7.Confirm and you’ll get a “Thank You” screen once the subscription is active

Is it worth it? Depends on your position size.

If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade.

For me, it’s a no-brainer.

text:
RARE EARTH THEME: Setting up Huge 

Rare earth elements are the "vitamins" of the 2026 technology buildout. You only need a small amount, but without them, the high-performance hardware required for AI, photonics, and space infrastructure simply won't function.

They are the PHSYICAL BOTTLENECK for AI, As of 2026, China still refines over 90% of the world's supply. This has created a "stockpiling" environment as the U.S. and E.U. rush to bring non-Chinese refining facilities (like MP Materials in California) online before new defense bans take effect in 2027.

The Time Gap: While a data center can be built in months, opening a new mine and refinery often takes a decade. This mismatch between skyrocketing demand and slow supply growth is the primary reason for the "rerate" we're about to see. 

When a entire group is setting up, you pay attention.

text:
LWLG — a real materials bet wrapped in a momentum stock NFA

Lightwave Logic makes Perkinamine electro-optic polymers that spin-coat onto silicon photonics wafers in a back-end-of-line step  (<100°C) to convert passive silicon waveguides into sub-volt, high-bandwidth Mach-Zehnder modulators. 

The technology’s figure of merit (Vπ·L ≈ 0.1 V·cm) is roughly an order of magnitude better than silicon plasma-dispersion modulators and competitive with thin-film lithium niobate. 

The relevance to AI is direct: 800G/1.6T/3.2T optical interconnects and co-packaged optics need exactly this — low-drive-voltage, 

BEOL-compatible, foundry-integrable modulators.
Commercial reality is far less mature. FY2025 revenue: $237 thousand   (yes, thousand) on a $2.45B market cap (P/S ~10,000x). Net loss $20.3M;  cash $69M  after  the December 2025 $35M offering at $3.10/share.  Management explicitly states no meaningful production revenue until 2027.   

The investor must accept this is a binary 2027 outcome. The bull case rests on GlobalFoundries PDK integration (March 2026), Tower Semiconductor tape-out, SilTerra/Luceda partnership, and four 

Fortune Global 500 customers in Stage 3 of the Design Win Cycle. Importantly, the Marvell-Polariton acquisition (closed 
February 2026,  ~$3.25B + $2.25B earnout)  creates a tantalizing but unconfirmed technical link — Polariton’s plasmonic-organic-hybrid demos historically used Perkinamine,  but Marvell has announced no LWLG license or supply agreement.  

Bulls are extrapolating; bears point out the company has been “near commercialization” since its PSI-TEC days in the 2000s.

Marvell acquired Polariton on April 22 — a company specializing in ultra-high-performance plasmonic modulators. This likely made POET redundant on the Optical Interposer side (Marvell now has its own advanced in-house solution). BUT we must not forget the bigger picture: Marvell still desperately needs a reliable light source (lasers + External Light Sources / ELS). Polariton only provides the modulator (the “switch” that turns the light on and off). It does NOT provide the actual light itself. This is exactly where Ayar Labs + Sivers come into play. Ayar Labs is significantly more advanced than most competitors with its mature SuperNova platform — already in advanced prototyping, with proven system-level integration and strong hyperscaler interest. Ayar’s SuperNova platform uses Sivers’ high-performance multi-wavelength laser arrays as the core light engine. Why Ayar Labs is significantly better positioned than most competitors: Its SuperNova platform is one of the most mature CPO/ELS solutions on the market. Already in advanced prototyping with strong hyperscaler interest. Recently dropped Lumentum and Macom to keep Sivers as its exclusive laser supplier — a major vote of confidence. BUT competition is here : Lumentum ($LITE) and Coherent ($COHR) → already nearly fully booked through 2027-2028. Smaller players like Casela, Scintil Photonics and Xscape → solid but limited in scale and capacity. Bottom line: The POET drama is hurting short-term sentiment and creating volatility across the entire photonic AI sector. However, the core $SIVE thesis remains fully intact. Jabil’s 1.6T LRO partnership (the big industrial manufacturing arm) is completely separate and unaffected. Ayar Labs stays very well positioned, and O-Net is also active. text:
Ever since I started covering photonics...

My comments were full of "$POET, $POET, $POET"

WHY? Me nor a single photonics trader I know ever thought anything of it.

Pre rev. Paid stock promo. Now wrecked trying to hype the stock then getting $MRVL mad.

Be careful buying a stock in a hot category just because "it hasn't ran yet". That's not an investment thesis.

Here’s the landscape: $MTSI – Builds high-performance analog chips that help move data efficiently inside networks $GLW – Makes the fiber and glass that physically carries data across the world $CIEN – Provides networking gear that powers high-speed data traffic $COHR – Develops lasers and photonics components used in AI and data center systems $FN – Manufactures optical hardware that connects and scales data centers $MRVL – Designs custom chips that act as the backbone for cloud and AI infrastructure $ALAB – Focused on connectivity chips solving data flow bottlenecks in AI clusters $AVGO – Supplies critical semis across networking, storage, and AI infrastructure $AEHR – Builds testing equipment for next-gen semiconductors and photonics devices $CRDO – Creates ultra-fast interconnect chips linking AI servers at scale $AAOI – Produces optical modules that enable high-speed data transmission $LITE – Designs optical components and lasers powering AI data center connectivity This isn’t just another AI theme. It’s the plumbing behind it. text:
On a day where data centers from $CIFR to $NBIS & photonics from $POET to $AAOI were pulling back strongly

Might make you think we're risk-off?

Except the rare earth/critical metals sector is greener than uranium

$USAR hit another +7% today erasing all of Friday gains as a sympathy play to $CRML's new acquisition

Can't believe it was trading for what the government paid for it last week.

$USAR up ~15% on the announcement to acquire Serra Verde for $2.8B in cash & stock

though the deal comes with dilution

looks like the market supports $USAR acquiring the only large-scale producer of heavy rare earths outside Asia? https://t.co/QKOGh9m1lb

text:
For long-term investments (3-5+ years) in photonics/AI data links, focus on proven moats + execution over pure hype:

1. **MRVL**: Best balance—custom AI silicon + optical interconnects, real hyperscaler revenue.
2. **TSM**: Unmatched scale as the foundry powering it all.
3. **AVGO**: Networking dominance with optical edge.
4. **ASML**: Lithography monopoly for advanced chips.

High-upside but riskier: POET/AAOI if their tech scales. AXTI/AEHR solid but more niche. DYOR, diversify.

text:
Photonics stocks ride AI's need for faster, cooler data links. All have tailwinds, but vary in moat, execution risk, and hype vs reality.

Research priority (upside + strategic edge first):

1. POET (+113% PT): Optical interposers could transform chip comms—highest reward/risk.
2. MRVL (+76%): Custom AI silicon + optics; proven hyperscaler traction.
3. AAOI (+68%): GPU transceivers; direct AI cluster play.
4. AXTI (+71%): Indium phosphide wafers; foundational material.
5. AEHR (+65%): Burn-in testing; scales reliability at volume.

Start here, then TSM/INTC/ASML as anchors. Smaller ones like LWLG/POET need deeper DD on tech viability.

text:
$LWLG Catalyst-Driven Thesis

Catalyst-driven setup accelerating in AI silicon photonics. Pre-revenue, but de-risking fast with real foundry + IP momentum. Optical interconnect bottleneck in data centers is real — low-power, high-bandwidth EO polymers targeting 200G/400G+ lanes.

Major Recent Catalysts :

• April 29, 2026: Engaged Michael Best & Friedrich LLP (Am Law 200) as strategic IP advisor. Focus: invention harvesting, patent fortress, international filings, portfolio management & licensing/tech transfer to foundries. Clear commercialization push.

• March 11, 2026: Development Agreement with Tower Semiconductor — integrating Perkinamine® polymers into PH18 SiPho platform. Multiple engineering tapeouts planned for 2026 targeting high-speed, low-power modulators.

• March 16, 2026: High-speed modulator platform now live in GDSFactory PDK for GlobalFoundries. Plug-and-play for designers & hyperscalers.
Fundamentals Snapshot (transparent):

•  Pre-revenue (~$237K in 2025) with typical development-stage losses.
•  Debt-free + solid cash runway into 2027+ via recent raise/ATM.
•  Active ATM (normal for this stage — funding R&D/tapeouts).
•  Insider sales mostly tax/option exercises post-run (standard, not dumping).
•  ~9-10% short interest, elevated but manageable. No new red flags or scandals.

Risks vs Reward: Execution on 2026 tapeouts/design wins critical. Commercial revenue targeted ~2027. High-volatility micro-cap story stock in a massive AI tailwind (optical transceivers market → $100B by 2030).

text: SOI is the fundamental layer of Silicon Photonics, and Soitec has IP moat
embedded_text:
Soitec $SOI / $SLOIF is up more than 370% YTD.
But step back to a 5-year view and the stock is still down 28.99%. So this is a recovery, not a breakout to new highs.

So what's behind the move?
SOI wafers feed a broad set of end markets (silicon photonics, RF, and others).

#DYNASTYProject #FORTH #Photonics #ResearchTraining #HorizonEurope https://t.co/Re6cZENNGe

The rally's still early in some layers. DYOR. 🚀

$SIVE $LPK $ALRIB $INFQ $LWLG 🔥
Here’s my conviction portfolio - I’ve invested the most in these five, because they sit at the epicenter of the exploding AI, photonics, quantum, and advanced manufacturing mega-trends

$SIVE – Sivers Semiconductors
Leading player in high-performance photonics lasers and mmWave wireless chips. Powering AI data centers, SATCOM, 5G/6G, and optical networks with game-changing efficiency. 

$LPK – LPKF Laser & Electronics
Precision laser systems for next-gen electronics manufacturing - PCB prototyping, laser welding, and advanced materials processing. The hidden enabler behind faster, cheaper, and more sustainable chip & component production.

$ALRIB – Riber
World leader in Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE) equipment - the critical tool for growing the compound semiconductor materials used in lasers, RF chips, VCSELs, and quantum devices.

$INFQ – Infleqtion
Pure-play quantum computing building neutral-atom quantum computers, precision sensors, quantum clocks, and cold-atom tech for defense, commercial, and government customers.

$LWLG – Lightwave Logic
Pioneering electro-optic polymers (Perkinamine) that deliver ultra-high-speed, low-power photonic chips. The polymer platform that could finally solve the bandwidth bottleneck in AI data centers and silicon photonics.

Did I missed something?

The thing I learned from bitcoin is that supply/demand imbalances don't get resolved overnight.

The same thing applies to AI.

There are shortages in GPUs, CPUs, data centers, power generation, photonics, chemicals, and much more.

These imbalances won't go away anytime soon.

text:
The most interesting pattern in AI photonics this year is not a fundamental story. It's a flow story. And it's already played out twice in the last six weeks. Something that popped up when I was doing research on $SIVE for an article that drops tomorrow.

$SOI Soitec. March 26: Morgan Stanley upgrades with photonics SOI as the bull case. April 22: they cross the 5% threshold and now hold 6.50% of the company, acquired through an off-market transaction. The same window saw multiple disclosed shorts from Two Sigma, Numeric, AHL Partners, and JPMorgan Asset Management.

$IQE. Up 323% in twelve months. Stockopedia tags it a Momentum Trap, trading 283% above its 200-day moving average. April 27: $MTSI MACOM leads an 81 million GBP strategic investment that ends IQE's strategic review.

The setup is the same in both names.

European compound semiconductor companies trade cheap on their home exchanges because local investors do not price the AI photonics optionality. US institutional desks notice. Analyst reports flag overvaluation. Retail and local holders sell into the perceived weakness. US institutions accumulate the float at the bottom of the shake-out. The fundamentals are not the trigger. The flow is.

Now look at $SIVE Sivers Semiconductors. Up over 900% in twelve months. Just announced a 1.6T transceiver collaboration with Jabil. Evaluating a Nasdaq New York dual listing. Stockopedia tags it a Momentum Trap. Analyst consensus 79% below market price.

Identical setup to where Soitec was in early March.

I want to be careful here. This is a hypothesis based on three data points. It tells you what could happen if the playbook repeats. It does not tell you when. It does not tell you whether the stock takes a 50% drawdown first. The companies are in different stages of maturity. Soitec has 890M EUR in revenue. Sivers has 30M USD, 72% of it NRE.

But the playbook is real. The receipts are public. It explains why analyst targets 80% below market, Momentum Trap classifications, and off-market transactions keep clustering in the same sub-sector at the same time.

I do not own any of these names. My framework is fundamentals, and I do not have edge in predicting where Morgan Stanley parks a 6.5% position three months from now. But the flow case is the most interesting non-fundamental thesis I have seen in AI infrastructure picks-and-shovels this year.

Worth watching closely.

No, Infleqtion and PsiQuantum are separate companies with different quantum tech approaches (neutral atoms vs. photonics).

Both are tenants at Chicago's Illinois Quantum & Microelectronics Park (IQMP). PsiQuantum is the anchor building a large photonic utility-scale system there. Infleqtion is developing its own neutral-atom quantum computer on the same campus as part of a $50M+ Illinois initiative. No direct affiliation between them.
@AIInvestorHQ Semiconductors and photonics are the future and there is nothing we can do about it

text:
Massive YTD so far for PHOTONICS: The next layer of the AI buildout.
AI isn't just a compute story anymore.
It's becoming a data movement problem.
And the market is starting to price that in.
Why photonics is moving
More GPUs → more nodes → more data moving between them.
Copper can't keep up. Too much heat, too much power, wrong distances.
Photonics is the fix. And the buildout is already underway.
What the data is showing
$AAOI revenue grew 34% YoY last quarter — not speculation, actual orders.
800G and 1.6T optical capacity is being expanded right now to meet hyperscaler demand.
Earnings this week are the tell
$LITE — May 5 (after close)
$COHR — May 6 (after close)
$AAOI — May 7
If guidance on 800G/1.6T demand holds or accelerates, this thesis gets legs into 2027.
If hyperscaler commentary disappoints — the chart above gets a reality check.
Watch the guidance, that will be the clue. 
#photonics

text:
Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a small-cap player with real momentum: FY2025 revenue SEK 304M (+25% YoY), $453M pipeline, and production ramps in AI photonics, LiDAR (potential $28-53M cumulative), and SATCOM/defense deals. It's 50x smaller than $LITE (~$68B mcap, $2.1B TTM rev) or $COHR (~$62B mcap, multi-billion scale).

That size difference creates higher torque if execution hits—but also higher risk: ongoing losses, execution hurdles, competition from scaled peers, and valuation sensitivity. The big caps are more mature with broader customer bases.

Data backs the growth case for SIVE, yet skeptics rightly note small-caps often fail to deliver. DYOR; this isn't advice.

"strategy of prioritizing bleeding-edge projects like photonics, nano-materials, and AI agents over saturated markets or outdated apps, believing it yields better long-term ROI and personal learning."  Yes, push the needle forward, we live in an age of disruption, go full-speed ahead on new exciting things, if it doesn't exist join or find like-minded people who can push the needle forward with you... Anduril is like one of those companies, and a few others that keep that startup/bright-eyed vibe instead of getting lost in corporate jargon.

We can push through so many hurdles right now and help others with disruptive tech! :)

text:
$XND U is Xanadu Quantum Technologies, a photonic quantum computing company building quantum computers with light-based qubits and optical networking.

It uses advanced photonics (some overlap with optical hardware tech), and has ties to players like Tower Semi ($TSEM). But it's not in the CPO optical interconnect supply chain for classical AI data center clusters (lasers, packaging, foundry like the $SIVE/Shunsin/MSS names).

Different play: speculative quantum vs. near-term optical networking supercycle for $NVDA/$AVGO.
@Craaazy1231 Yeah and the thing is it’s just starting lol as this photonics cycle will continue until 2030 atleast.

text:
🚀 $SIVE Price Targets: Week / Month / Year (Top 3 Catalysts Driving It)

$SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) has absolutely exploded — up ~815% YTD and trading at ~38 SEK after hitting a 52-week high of 44.70 SEK. This photonics/laser play (wireless, optical comms, LiDAR, CPO for AI data centers) is riding serious momentum. Here’s my take on realistic price targets based on the top 3 near-term catalysts:

1. Nasdaq New York Dual Listing (Biggest Near-Term Catalyst)
•  Announced April 16, 2026 — they’re doing the PCAOB audit uplift and delayed the 2025 annual report to May 15 to prep for it (while staying domiciled in Sweden).
•  This opens the floodgates to US tech capital, higher liquidity, and a potential valuation rerating (US multiples love photonics/AI plays).
•  Impact: Listing progress/news could be the spark.

2. Major LiDAR Ramp (Q4 2026 Production Start)
•  Strategic customer integrating Sivers’ lasers + optical amplifiers across multiple platforms.
•  Revenue potential: $53M–$138M cumulative over the product lifecycle (auto + industrial). Ramp begins Q4 2026.
•  This is real, high-volume revenue coming online.

3. CPO / AI Data Center Supercycle (Longer-Term Rocket Fuel)
•  Sivers is a leader in external laser sources for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
•  Recent Jabil collab on 1.6T LRO transceivers for AI data centers + 64% pipeline growth to $453M. Tied directly into the NVDA/AVGO optical boom.
My speculative price targets (NFA — this thing is volatile as hell, high P/S, execution risk):

•  Next Week: 40–45 SEK (Momentum carry + any listing/earnings buzz. Watch for pullbacks after the recent spike.)

•  Next Month: 50–65 SEK (Dual-listing updates + continued AI/photonics hype. Could test new highs if volume stays strong.)

•  Next Year (End 2026 / into 2027): 90–130+ SEK (Dual listing complete + LiDAR ramp kicking off + CPO orders scaling. Market cap could easily 2–3x from here on execution.)

This is a high-conviction, high-risk name — asymmetric upside if the catalysts hit, but it’s already priced in a ton of optimism. Fundamentals are still catching up (still loss-making), but the narrative is strong in the AI/optics supercycle.

Position size accordingly and DYOR. What’s your $SIVE target? 

Holding long or swinging the volatility? 🔥

(Prices in SEK. Current ~38 as of late April close. Not financial advice.)

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "244278", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2048853695010365707", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "827", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The CPU bottleneck could be bigger than photonics.

Because it only gets worse as AI chips get better.

A Georgia Tech/$INTC paper makes it clear. The researchers hooked up real agentic AI workloads (research assistants, coding agents, web-search agents) and stopped to watch every step.

On a cheaper GPU setup, the CPU caused 38% of the total wait time. Then they ran the same workloads on an H200. The CPU's share jumped to 65%.

A faster GPU just means you finish the GPU computations sooner, which makes the CPU wait for all the agentic actions look even bigger by comparison.

Every accelerator upgrade only sends buyers back to order more CPUs...

This is exactly why $INTC CFO said unfulfilled demand for CPUs starts with a B.

More deep research into the CPU bottleneck in me and @KawzInvests Substack deep dive, really worked to help folks wrap their head around it in a digestible format.

$AMD $INTC $ARM $QCOM $TSM

if healthcare doesn’t change in a hospital 🏥 first… …but on your wrist? $SIVE / $SIVEF 🍎🩺 That’s why I keep coming back to $AAPL and Sivers. This is bigger than “just another sensor.” This is about turning the Apple Watch into something far more powerful than a smartwatch, a health guardian people wear every single day. Blood pressure is one step. But the real prize is glucose monitoring. A future where millions of people no longer have to rely on needles, strips, and constant friction to stay on top of their health. A future where wearables help monitor the body quietly, continuously, in the background. And the opportunity goes beyond that. Not just blood pressure. Not just glucose.  Potentially hydration, recovery, respiratory-related signals, temperature-linked changes, and even sensing different things in the air around us. That’s when the story changes. The watch stops being a gadget. It becomes a guardian. And the need is massive: Diabetes already affects roughly 1 in 9 adults globally, with more than 40% undiagnosed. By 2050, that number is expected to reach 853 million. Now connect that with what Sivers has already disclosed:  follow-on design optimization work and ~50,000 laser units for its first established U.S. Fortune 100 customer, after an earlier 30,000-unit order. That’s how something world-changing often starts. Quietly.  With testing, iteration, and validation. My own view: I think this launches in 2028. And if that’s right, pre-series orders 12–18 months before launch would make a lot of sense. Now imagine Apple mass-producing health-focused consumer hardware at 50M+ units per year. That’s not just massive for Sivers.  That’s massive for the future of healthcare. embedded_text:
It's interesting how the people most worried about the SOH/energy crisis are also most skeptical of AI.

On the other hand, the most AI-pilled are dismissive of the SOH.

It's all a big Rorschach test...

text:
🚀 El sector de Ópticas / Photonics lidera las subidas hoy:

•  $VIAV +15.95%
•  $LWLG +12.84%
•  $AXTI +10.88%
•  $CIEN +9.49%
•  $AEHR +9.48%
•  $GLW +7.21%
•  $LITE +6.72%
•  $POET +6.67%
•  $AAOI +5.22%
•  $COHR +4.22% 
Todas estas compañías forman parte del superciclo de photonics: la infraestructura óptica crítica para los data centers de IA (transceivers de alta velocidad, láseres InP, silicon photonics y testing).

Nota: $AXTI publica resultados esta noche después del cierre (EPS esperado: -0,07$; Ingresos ~26,2 Mn$).

El momentum en la cadena de suministro óptica de la IA sigue intacto.

¿Qué opinas? ¿Continuará el rally o es solo rotación?

text:
$AEHR It's been interesting to watch the stock the past week, following the pullback which $POET kicked off in the silicon photonics space.

$TER reported on Tuesday. Great numbers, but the stock dropped by double digits yesterday; a drop that $AEHR didn't participate much in. Teradyne is up again today.

Is the stock ahead of itself? Yes, but it's tough to fade this rally. The acceleration is clear, and it sounds like there's a strong chance of additional orders landing in May per the CEO. How much of that is priced in? We'll find out next month.

text:
Jensen's 5-Layer AI framework is the cleanest investment thesis you'll see in 2026. Energy. Compute. Photonics. Memory. Chips. Every layer is in shortage. Every layer is repricing higher.

Layer 1: Energy. AI training campuses pulling 1+ gigawatts each. Data center power demand growing 40 percent annually. Bloom Energy, Oklo, Vertiv. Bull thesis is small modular reactors and fuel cells get the AI buildout contracts. Bear thesis is grid backlogs delay the buildout 3 years.

Layer 2: Compute. Hyperscaler GPU clusters can't keep up. CoreWeave, Nebius, IREN, Cipher. The neoclouds capture overflow demand at premium pricing. Bull case is 5x revenue. Bear case is hyperscalers self-supply and squeeze them out.

Layer 3: Photonics. Networking inside data centers is the new bottleneck. Applied Optoelectronics, Lumentum, Coherent, Astera Labs. 800G and 1.6T optical transceivers sold out through 2027. Bull case is networking captures real margin. Bear case is hyperscalers vertically integrate optics.

Layer 4: Memory. The bottleneck inside the bottleneck. Micron, SanDisk, Seagate, plus SK Hynix and Samsung internationally. DA Davidson just put a $1,000 PT on Micron. Bull case is structurally extended cycle. Bear case is China NAND oversupply.

Layer 5: Chips. NVDA, AMD, Broadcom, plus custom silicon at Google, Amazon, Meta. Bull case is NVDA's CUDA moat holds 5 more years. Bear case is custom silicon takes 30 percent share by 2028 and the stack price-compresses.

What nobody's saying: this isn't a "buy everything AI" framework.

It's a structural map of where shortages compound. Every layer has supply tight through 2027. Pick the layer with the deepest moat and the cheapest valuation.

My answer: memory at Layer 4. Energy at Layer 1.

The map is real.

Pick your shovels.

Layer 2: Compute. Hyperscaler GPU clusters can't keep up. CoreWeave, Nebius, IREN, Cipher. The neoclouds capture overflow demand at premium pricing. Bull case is 5x revenue. Bear case is hyperscalers self-supply and squeeze them out.

Layer 3: Photonics. Networking inside data centers is the new bottleneck. Applied Optoelectronics, Lumentum, Coherent, Astera Labs. 800G and 1.6T optical transceivers sold out through 2027. Bull case is networking captures real margin. Bear case is hyperscalers vertically integrate optics.

Layer 4: Memory. The bottleneck inside the bottleneck. Micron, SanDisk, Seagate, plus SK Hynix and Samsung internationally. DA Davidson just put a $1,000 PT on Micron. Bull case is structurally extended cycle. Bear case is China NAND oversupply.

Layer 5: Chips. NVDA, AMD, Broadcom, plus custom silicon at Google, Amazon, Meta. Bull case is NVDA's CUDA moat holds 5 more years. Bear case is custom silicon takes 30 percent share by 2028 and the stack price-compresses.

What nobody's saying: this isn't a "buy everything AI" framework.

It's a structural map of where shortages compound. Every layer has supply tight through 2027. Pick the layer with the deepest moat and the cheapest valuation.

My answer: memory at Layer 4. Energy at Layer 1.

The map is real.

Pick your shovels.

today: 
"wins a $21M Texas grant to expand semiconductor and photonics manufacturing in Sugar Land.  The project adds a 210K sq. ft. facility for AI data center transceiver production and is expected to create 500+ jobs." https://t.co/wKWAy76O36

Foxconn Is Ready to Embrace CPO, as Silicon Photonic Products Manage to Reach 3.2T Transmission Capacity 

On the 29th, Guo Haozhong, director of the Foxconn Semiconductor Research Institute, delivered a speech on silicon photonics trends at the 2026 MIC FORUM Spring "Intelligent New Order" seminar. He said that Foxconn's (2317) silicon photonics products can already achieve a transmission capacity of 3.2T, but with the continued shortage of InP capacity, silicon photonics will still face 10 challenges to overcome in the future.

This year, the slogan for AI servers has changed from "fiber to copper" to "fiber and copper in parallel". This is mainly because Broadcom chips have optimized the 200G transmission speed. However, copper wires will become increasingly difficult to use, especially high-speed copper wires which are heavy and require external sheathing. The advantage of optical communication is that a single wire can carry multiple wavelengths simultaneously without interfering with each other, up to a maximum of eight wavelengths.

Guo Haozhong stated that Foxconn's modulator can already achieve a single-channel transmission speed of 200G, using 2 or 4 different wavelength lasers and employing WDM (wavelength division multiplexing) technology to integrate on a PIC (photonic integrated circuit) chip; for a single-channel 200G, if two groups of four wavelengths are used, a transmission capacity of 3.2T can be achieved, and it even has the ability to upgrade the technology to 6.4T in the future.
Since future data center transmission speeds will reach over 400G, he stated that the current single-channel transmission rate of silicon photonics is limited to nearly 200G and has high power requirements. InP has advantages in high-speed transmission, not only integrating lasers but also helping to support transmission of systems above 400G. However, InP material is not as stable as silicon, resulting in low yield and high cost. Currently, due to insufficient production capacity, many companies are also investing in the research and development of other materials, such as TFLN or SOH, which have become materials of interest.

InP has advantages in high-speed transmission, not only integrating lasers but also helping to support transmission of systems above 400G. {"from_index": 2228, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2257}, {"from_index": 2423, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2467}]}, "text": "I think one of the most underestimated parts of the Sivers / $SIVE story is the SATCOM opportunity with https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp. This is not just another customer relationship. Sivers has been working with https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp for years through several development programs worth many millions of dollars, and the relationship appears to go much deeper than a normal supplier agreement. Sivers is part of the technology roadmap for advanced satellite terminals that are built for a world where connectivity will move across different orbits, networks and operators. That is where the potential becomes interesting. According to $SIVE own material, a SATCOM terminal can contain up to around 1,800 Sivers chips, with potential Sivers value of up to around $10,000 per terminal. That changes the way you should think about scale. This is not a consumer hardware story where millions of units are needed before revenue matters. In this case, even relatively small deployment numbers can become meaningful very quickly. If https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp sells 1,000 terminals, that is up to around $10 million for Sivers. At 5,000 terminals, up to around $50 million. At 10,000 terminals, up to around $100 million. And that is from one customer platform. This is why I think the market may be underestimating SATCOM. The focus is often on when Wireless can become meaningful again, but the answer may not only come from huge 5G volumes. It could also come from a smaller number of high-value satellite terminals where Sivers has very high value per terminal. The timing also matters. $SIVE has indicated that volume ramp is expected in H2 2026. If that happens, SATCOM moves from development revenue into something much more important: recurring product revenue tied to actual terminal deployments. And the backdrop is strong. https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp is already working with demanding customers and partners such as the US Army, NATO-related defence programs, Viasat, SES and Intelsat, while defence demand for resilient satellite communication is increasing and commercial networks are moving toward more advanced multi-orbit connectivity. Terminals need to become smarter, more capable and more complex. That is exactly where Sivers beamforming technology becomes valuable. For me, this is one of the most interesting parts of the $SIVE story into 2026. Not because it requires massive volumes. But because in SATCOM, small volumes can already become big numbers. #SATCOM #Beamforming #SatelliteCommunications #SpaceTech #DefenceTech #MultiOrbit #SIVEF"}}}, "rest_id": "2049351404595163589", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "18094", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2049540957603831884", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "631", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Laser Photonics $LASE, yaklaşık 5.7 milyon adet varantın $0.70 fiyatla kullanılmasıyla ~4 milyon dolar nakit elde etti. 

Bunun karşılığında yatırımcılara, $0.975 kullanım fiyatlı yeni varantlar (Series A-5 ve A-6) verildi.

 Bu yeni varantlar hissedar onayı sonrası devreye girecek ve 2–5 yıl vadeli olacak.

text:
Arrogance is costly.

Some of the highly regarded Korean photonics crew are about to get punished… because they’re still looking at charts on a 15-minute / yearly basis.

https://t.co/IIoZNOe7gU

“Price is king” is a lesson they still need to learn.
Warned this guy earlier (tweet below).
But we all learn at our own pace.

The real lesson: solid analyst teams and influencers with great picks, but limited experience pumping overbought stocks to their followers right before a 40–50% correction.

$IQE is headed to the 23–28 range.
We’ll be buyers then.

text:
We will be taking a short position in Semis here.
Short trade expected to last a couple of weeks. After that, we’ll look to pick up bottoms in select photonics names following a solid correction/profit-taking phase.

Trade setup:
- Using SMH, SPXL, and SOXS
- Earnings pop into mega tech earnings today
- Ideal entry on SOXS in the $13.60–$14.25 range
- Targeting 25–30% return over the next 2 weeks

Some photonic stocks will likely sell off hard (some already started) - which should create a good buying opportunity for those who missed the big rally.

Stay tuned.

text:
The Full Optical AI Industry Chain:  

1. IC Design & Manufacturing  
(Where the brains are built)

ASIC / xPU / Photonic ICs  
→ $NVDA $MRVL $AVGO $LITE $COHR $INTC $AMD $CSCO  

Wafer Foundries  
→ $TSM $TSEM $GFS $UMC  

2. Materials (The Overlooked Layer)  
(The foundation of photonics)  

Indium Phosphide  
→ $AXTI $IQE  

Gallium Arsenide  
→ $AXTI $MTSI  

This layer is underfollowed and could see strong upside as optical demand expands.

3. Optical Components  
(The data highways)  

VCSEL / EML Lasers  
→ $LITE $COHR $AVGO $AAOI  

Optical Module Assembly  
→ $FN $COHR $LITE $MRVL $CSCO  

Fiber / WDM / Optical Connectivity  
→ $CIEN $GLW $LITE  

MPO Connectors  
→ $GLW $APH  

Optical Fiber  
→ $GLW  

4. Packaging & Testing  
(The hidden backbone)  

Packaging  
→ $ASX $AMKR $FN $JBL  

Packaging Equipment  
→ $KLIC 

Testing  
→ $TER $KEYS $FORM $AEHR

As AI clusters scale into hundreds of thousands of GPUs, the challenge becomes moving data faster, farther, and with less power. Copper is hitting its limits. Optical is the next layer of scale. Why optical matters: • Higher bandwidth • Lower latency • Lower power consumption • Less heat • Better long-distance transmission That’s why hyperscalers are aggressively investing in optical-heavy infrastructure. Core optical AI plays on my radar: $COHR — broad optical exposure $LITE — datacenter optical leader $AAOI — optical modules growth $FN — manufacturing backbone $MRVL — AI networking silicon $CIEN — optical transport infrastructure AI compute gets the attention. The picks and shovels play just moved from chips to light.

✅ قائمة المراقبة الدورية للـ AI (النسخة الكاملة)

يهذي الدورة الكبرى للذكاء الاصطناعي اللي لسى معظم الناس ما انتبهوا لها:

السيميكوندكترز (Semis): $NVDA $AMD $ARM $AVGO $MRVL $INTC $TSM $QCOM

الذاكرة (Memory): $MU $WDC $STX $SNDK

الفوتونيكس والألياف البصرية (Photonics / Optical): $LITE $COHR $AAOI $GLW $JBL $AXTI $LWLG $NOK

الشبكات (Networking): $ANET $CSCO $CIEN $JNPR

الحوسبة ومراكز البيانات (Compute / Data Centers): $IREN $CIFR $NBIS

الطاقة والشبكة الكهربائية (Power / Grid): $VRT $ETN $PWR $GEV $CEG $SMR $OKLO

المواد والمعادن (Materials / Minerals): $MP $LAC $UUUU $USAR

الفضاء والأقمار الصناعية (Space / Satellites): $ASTS $RKLB $LUNR $PL $BKSY

الدفاع والدرونز (Defense / Drones): $AVAV $KTOS $ONDS $OSS
الروبوتات والـ AI (Robotics / AI): $TSLA $PATH $SYM

هذا بالضبط مسار تدفق الفلوس ودورانها في هالسوبر سايكل.

تابعوا الدورة وما تتأخروا عن القطار 🚀

text:
$LWLG is poised for explosive growth with an unmatched competitive edge that positions the company as a clear leader in next-generation photonics for AI-driven data centers and high-speed communications. 

With over 70 issued U.S. and international patents plus dozens more pending, they enjoy complete bulletproof freedom-to-operate across their entire suite of electro-optic polymer materials and devices, eliminating any risk of IP challenges and safeguarding their path to market dominance. 

Their state-of-the-art in-house manufacturing operation right in Denver, Colorado, produces rare-earth-free, fully supply-chain-resilient polymers that are already scaled and ready for high-volume production, giving them total control over quality and eliminating the geopolitical vulnerabilities that plague competitors reliant on overseas critical materials. 

Now in 2026, the company has successfully completed its full transition from pure R&D into commercialization mode, laser-focused on delivering the exceptional reliability and thermal stability required for seamless integration into leading foundry processes like Silicon Photonics. 

At the core of this breakthrough are their proprietary Perkinamine polymers, which deliver superior electro-optic performance with blazing bandwidths beyond 200 Gbps per lane, dramatically lower power consumption, and industry-first reliability metrics that have passed rigorous Telcordia GR-468 testing. 

This powerful combination of ironclad intellectual property, domestic manufacturing independence, and polymers engineered for real-world foundry deployment creates a formidable moat that will drive significant market penetration and shareholder value as demand for ultra-efficient optical interconnects surges in the AI era.

text:
Long list but concentration varies wildly across these categories. 
CW lasers for silicon photonics have maybe 3-4 companies that can deliver at volume. Passive optical components have dozens of interchangeable suppliers. Goldman's report lists them all the same. The investment case is very different depending on which layer actually has pricing power.

text: Nailed it! AI-driven due diligence spots management red flags instantly. Photonics supply chains need bulletproof governance, not influencer hype! 🤖📉
embedded_text:
$POET shares are down ~45% today after $MRVL canceled all Celestial AI purchase orders, citing alleged confidentiality breaches. 

On last Friday's Futurum Equities Podcast, @StockSavvyShay flagged $POET as one photonics name he wouldn't touch, pointing to management red flags https://t.co/xPBUhyyZc7

text:
In the analogy, replicating POET's specialized "steaming/packing" (their patented Optical Interposer for hybrid photonics integration) would take Marvell 3-5+ years: R&D, machinery/fab quals, chef training (process validation), and supply chain scaling. 

Not utility-max: Marvell inherited the Celestial AI/POET supply relationship (confirmed PO now shipping Q3), focuses on ASICs/networking, and just bought Polariton for optics scale. They have choices but prefer faster AI time-to-market over verticalizing rare blue lobster tech right now.
@Jamesdchoang838 Photonics took a hit, but we have $CLS earnings this week. Lets see.

text:
From engaging with global leadership at the IEEE TAB meeting in Austin to connecting with peers and industry leaders at the IEEE Photonics Board of Governors leadership meeting and the Optical Fiber Communication Conference 2026, the latest President’s Column by Gabriella Bosco underscores two key themes: the powerful energy of a truly global community and the growing importance of collaboration across disciplines.

In this article, she reflects on her recent travels and shares her favorite part of serving as President—celebrating the people behind the Society’s progress. From award recipients to dedicated volunteers, this community continues to inspire.

Learn More: https://t.co/LzFVo9qD05

#IEEE #Photonics #Leadership #IEEEAwards #STEMed #Innovation

@ofcconference @IEEE_TA

$iqe 

Raise happened in March, hence the 19p price. No one knew stock would 3x in April

18% dilution, so ignore 19p

No debt 

Stronger operating business. 20% growth guide for 2026 likely means 40% photonics gth 

Order book gets stronger in 2H, ie 2027 is accelerating https://t.co/TBddnqVlOj

1. $MTSI invest £45M via equity + CLN + multi-yr LTA for epi wafers
2. Institutional placing + offer to existing UK retail shareholders at 19.8p (58% discount)
3. Participation from existing convertible noteholders (~£23M reinvested)

Proceeds repay bank debt, https://t.co/yGWoXWRAJv

Participation from existing convertible noteholders (~£23M reinvested) Proceeds repay bank debt, redeem loan notes and fund core tech/capex. $MTSI say - "We believe this transaction will strengthen our supply chain resilience, while positioning $IQE to improve its balance sheet and financial performance across its business” What it means for $IQE: - operating biz is materially stronger: debt-free(er) & LTA-backed utilisation ramp via $MTSI - once the dilution digests & 2027 numbers show margin expansion from higher utilisation, $IQE can re-rate on visible AI-driven rev growth - near-term, things will be volatile (lower share price) - but the fundamental derisking is pretty huge - plus, wider AI tailwinds remain My view: - overall positive - textbook capital raise for an AI supply chain play - retail tranche shows thoughtful shareholder management = positive signal on management - $MTSI partnership + clean balance sheet outweigh the dilution - acts as a de-risking catalyst - dilution is the price paid for removing the debt overhang + securing commercial upside Imo this $MTSI partnership de-risks $IQE as a critical point in the supply chain quite significantly. If you're looking to enter for the first time, I'd suggest waiting for the dips over the next few days/weeks first. I have a very low cost basis - so I’m maintaining my full position & will add on any incoming dips."}}}, "rest_id": "2048812612628931057", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "84568", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2048828731725078830", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1172", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}
